title: Class methods should be preferred instead of prototype assignments
description: Class methods should be used instead of "prototype" assignmentsAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode Smelles2015type-dependentWhy is this an issue?Originally JavaScript didn’t supportclasses, and class-like behavior had to be kludged using things likeprototypeassignments for "class" functions. Fortunately, ECMAScript 2015 added classes, so any lingeringprototypeuses should be converted to trueclasses. The new syntax is more expressive and clearer, especially to those with experience in other languages.Specifically, with ES2015, you should simply declare aclassand define its methods inside the class declaration.Noncompliant code examplefunction MyNonClass(initializerArgs = []) { this._values = [...initializerArgs]; } MyNonClass.prototype.doSomething = function () { // Non
recommendation: Replace with the recommended alternative approach.

checks.message: Use class methods instead of prototype assignments for better ES2015+ compatibility.
